name: Ma'ili Pillbox (Pink Pillbox)
tagline: The West Side's colorful pillbox hike with sunset views that absolutely crush the East Side versions.
parking: Free parking at the base trailhead on Ma'ili Point — small gravel lot
bestTime: Late afternoon for the best sunset — West Side faces the ocean sunset directly
talkStory: About 1.5 miles round trip with 500 ft of quick elevation gain — short and steep but totally doable for most people. The two pillboxes at the top are covered in colorful graffiti art (hence 'Pink Pillbox') and offer 360-degree views of the Waianae Coast, Makaha Valley, and the open ocean. This is the best sunset pillbox hike on Oahu, hands down — the sun drops right into the Pacific in front of you. The trail is steep and loose in spots, so wear proper shoes. Leeward side means it's almost always dry and sunny.
